title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Data Contract Brief: Audit Provenance
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql audit provenance increment in a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.
Minimize collected fields, purpose, access, retention, export, and re-identification risk across the lifecycle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-audit-provenance — Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.
-- Application contract: prepare once and bind :severity as data.
SELECT ticket_id, title, status
FROM tickets
WHERE severity = 'high'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Structural choices such as sort direction require an application allow-list.
